observations.recommendation:
1.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to make the necessary adjustments to correct the misstatements of the Power
Supply Systems and Office Equipment accounts in the year-end financial
statements.

2.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the error in
recording the foregoing transactions and reflect the accurate balances of Due to
NGAs, Due to LGUs and Trust Liabilities accounts in the financial statements as
at year end.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to verify and adjust all book reconciling items found/noted to reflect the correct
balance of Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements as at year end.

4.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the following:

a. The Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR or Taxpayers Index
Card, and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant a certified list
showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
year. The certified list shall be updated and regularly submitted to the
Municipal Accountant every beginning of the year.

b. The Municipal Accountant to record the RPT and SET Receivables at the
beginning of the year based on the certified list furnished by the Municipal
Treasurer in order to record in the books not just mere estimates from
real property taxes but actual receivables from the Taxpayers as required
under Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

5.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a) The GSO-designate to ensure that all issuances of inventory to end-
users are properly supported with Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) and to
prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) on a
weekly basis.

b) The Municipal Accountant and GSO-designate to maintain Supplies
Ledger Card and Stock Cards, respectively, for each type of supplies
to record all receipts and issuances.

c) The Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of
inventories; prepare the required Report on the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than
July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively.

6.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. The Municipal Accountant to furnish the Audit Team with subsidiary
schedules of PPE and schedule of depreciation charges to facilitate the
verification of the computation of the depreciation expenses of all
depreciable assets;

b. The Municipal Accountant and GSO-designate to investigate and
reconcile immediately the discrepancies between the PPE balances per
accounting records and RPCPPE of the Inventory Committee;

c. Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Inventory Committee to
undertake collaborative procedure to ensure that all PPEs included in the
RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records; and,

d. The Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to maintain and
update the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards,
respectively, and regularly reconcile with the General Ledger and
Inventory Report to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE
accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU’s PPEs.

7.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to provide an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets owned by the
municipality, and to reflect the correct net book value of the Other Land
Improvements, Infrastructure Assets, and Buildings and Other Structures
accounts.

8.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the current year and prior year unexpended
QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

9.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the required reconciliations and to
provide in the Notes to Financial Statements the appropriate information
and/or disclosures required for the accounts and account balances presented
in the Financial Statements.

10.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer and the other implementing offices concerned to ensure that all
development projects to be funded under 20% DF are well-planned and
procurement-and-implementation ready. We further recommend that all
unimplemented PPAs be immediately implemented for the benefits of the
constituents.

11.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Property Officer
and other officials concerned to prepare IIRUP, create Appraisal and Disposal
Committee and initiate proper disposal procedures pursuant to Section 79 of PD
No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration of the properties. Proper segregation
must be made of these properties that are still saleable and which can be
subjected to destruction.

12.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Ensure that all cash advances, except for petty cash, should be fully
liquidated at the end of each year, and observe strict adherence to the
rules governing the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
and,

b. Send letters to employees to demand the immediate liquidation or refund
of their outstanding cash advances.

13.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. The Municipal Accountant to (i) process only those claims that are
properly documented; and (ii) impose collection of liquidated damages
from the suppliers who incurred delays in deliveries.

b. BAC Chairman to ensure that the terms and conditions indicated in the
contracts are strictly complied with.

14.4. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons/officers
responsible for the settlement of audit suspension to submit the necessary
documents as required in the Notices of Suspension issued. Non-enforcement
of settlement would otherwise lead to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance on
the pertinent transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to reclassify the liabilities as to current and non-current and
present the same accordingly in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Transfer the current year unexpended QRF and DRRMF- MOOE to the
Special Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end;
and
b. Return the remaining balance of DRRMF-CO to the General Fund and
refrain from transferring the unexpended/unobligated balance of
DRRM-Capital Outlay from General Fund to the Special Trust Fund
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the required reconciliations and present the same
either on the face of the financial statements, or as a disclosure in the Notes
to Financial Statements for a more complete and transparent Financial
Statement presentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on
the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) not
later than January 31 of each year; and,
b) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain property Cards (PC) for
all properties and equipment owned by the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive undertake the following
courses of action:
a. Direct the concerned department heads of the municipality to implement
the prior years’ unimplemented fund transfer and to follow-up the
submission of supporting documents in order to liquidate the funds; and,
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to refund the excess amounts of fund
transfers to the various source agencies or to the Bureau of Treasury, for
the National Government to reprogram the excess funds to other priority
PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure in the Notes
to Financial Statements and maintain subsidiary ledgers by year of
fund transfer for the Trust Liability-DRRM account pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2012-002; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amounts and balances of
LDRRM Fund presented in the utilization report and Notes to
Financial Statements are consistent with the source
documents/registries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the respective District
Supervisors to submit fund utilization report on the SEF share immediately
after the completion of the program, projects or activities. Otherwise, no
additional or subsequent releases shall be made to said schools and the
pertinent SEF transactions shall be completely processed in the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following
courses of action:
a. Direct the MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to verify the lacking
details of loan releases such as, name of borrower and date of release;
and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide relevant explanatory note
of all the accounts in the Financial Statement for a clearer information
for the users.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation for the GAD Focal
Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the
implementation of GAD related program and projects in order to attain the
objective for which the funds were provided.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinances with the
assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of
Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed through local policy-
making and integrated in the local development plans.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books to correct erroneous recording or classification of items in PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 reclassification Finding was complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the correcting accounting entries to the Audit Team for verification purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR The necessary accounting adjusting entry was made. We further recommended that a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) be executed between the LGU and the schools that will require the latter to submit Fund Utilization Reports to ensure that the funds transferred were utilized for the specified purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting accounting entry.
observations.managementAction: AAR The necessary accounting adjusting entry was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a) identify the breakdown of P748,760.30 balance from CY 2019 and prior years’ transactions; b) remit to the BIR the withholding taxes from all sources, regularly and intact, to avoid the accumulation of the same at year-end; and c) match the BIR remittances against withholdings recorded in the General Ledger and Journals on a monthly basis to prevent under or over-remittances of taxes.
observations.managementAction: AAR The audit recommendation is complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson, in coordination with BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking documentary requirements, in compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020, respectively.
observations.managementAction: AAR The lacking documentary requirements were already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Sangguniang Bayan Presiding Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124 dated March 26, 2020 and to Budget Operations Manual for LGUs in effecting changes in the annual budget to fund COVID-19-related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: AAR There was already strict adherence to rules and regulations in effecting changes to the annual budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to submit the following: a) Pictures taken during the receipt of the SAP by the beneficiaries; b) Original or certified true copies of the certifications issued by the respective Punong Barangays; c) Relevant document evidencing the inclusion to beneficiaries; and d) Duly dated, signed, and notarized Memorandum of Agreement by and between the municipality and DSWD Regional Director.
observations.managementAction: AAR The necessary documents were submitted. Pictures taken during the receipt of the SAP by the beneficiaries; Certifications by the respective PBs were submitted to the audit team. The necessary Memorandum of Agreement was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENRO Designate to enforce proper handling of collected wastes at the central Materials Recovery Facility, and maintain an efficient and safe storage of the same pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003 for the protection of the environment, the general public and persons directly handling the solid waste in the facility.
observations.managementAction: AAR Proper handling of collected wastes was enforced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek appropriate funding for the titling the said parcel of land and include it on their annual or supplemental budget.
observations.managementAction: AAR There was sufficient funding for titling the lots. The necessary documents were supported. The Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of title of these lots in favor of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to: a) Ensure that the cost of signboards was included in the project cost, provided such conform to the specifications set forth under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and b) Require the contractor to post signboard, necessary tarpaulins or similar materials as soon as the award has been made and must be suitably framed for outdoor display at the project location.
observations.managementAction: AAR Ensure signboards were incorporated in the project cost. Contractors were required to post the required signboard.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise due actions: a.) Require all drivers of motor vehicles to properly fill-in the entries required in the Driver's Trip Tickets and to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels for each motor vehicle; b.) Direct the personnel in-charge to serially number the Driver's Trip Tickets issued and prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle; and c.) Require the Bobon PNP to prepare and submit Driver's Trip Tickets and Monthly Report of Official Travels to the Municipality to monitor the fuel consumptions of their patrol cars.
observations.managementAction: AAR Paid DVs were stamped “Paid”; The necessary monthly report was prepared. Drivers trip tickets were not yet properly and completely filled in. While, the required monthly report was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to facilitate the implementation of PPAs funded by the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR The LDRRMO was directed to facilitate implementation of PPAs. There was noticeable and substantial implementation of LDRRMF-related PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the District Supervisor to optimize the utilization of Special Education Fund (SEF) Budget pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 (and Addenda), and to ensure SEF is utilized in accordance with guidelines.
observations.managementAction: AAR The CY 2021 SEF Budget was optimally utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to install measures necessary to safeguard the commercial units from theft, vandals and other elements.
observations.managementAction: AAR There were measures in place to protect the commercial units from theft, vandals and other elements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: a. To direct the Municipal Treasurer to confirm with Land Bank of the Philippines as to the status of LBP Account No. 1192-1230-93; b. To direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully prepare bank reconciliations by properly applying the correct accounting treatment of bank/book reconciling items in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles; and c. To prepare adjusting entries to record all book reconciling items which were not taken up at CY 2019.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Treasurer reported the status of account. The Municipal Accountant was directed to prepare the adjusting entries to record stale checks. This audit recommendation is complied.
observations.reason: Part of the outstanding balance of cash in bank remained due to stale checks which were identified and will be adjusted; outstanding items were being addressed with the bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action: a.) Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to strictly monitor and carefully evaluate the implementation of the PPAs for which the fund transfers were intended for; b.) Direct the Municipal Engineer to administer, supervise and control the implementation of the PPAs related to engineering and public works and establish close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator; c.) Direct the concerned Department Heads in the municipality to have close coordination with Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer regarding to the PPAs related to their respective offices; d.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to explain the non-liquidation of fully utilized fund transfers and to liquidate accordingly those fund transfers whose PPAs were already completed; and e.) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to determine the availability of cash of all fund transfers which are reported in the books and issue the check in favor of the Source Agencies to return all unused amount. In case the source agency could no longer be identified, the check shall be made in favor of the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Engineer and other concerned offices were directed to monitor and implement PPAs and to coordinate. The Municipal Accountant explained the non-liquidation and liquidated accordingly. The Municipal Treasurer determined availability of cash and processed returns.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The LDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the same within the reglementary period; and b) The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund; pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR The LDRRMO was directed to prepare and submit the monthly report. The necessary coordination was in place. The necessary explanation was made. Prepared monthly report was submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor to fully implement the 3rd General Revision and update every 3 years thereafter, pursuant to Section 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Assessor is implementing and adopting the CY 2012 3rd General Revision of Real Property Units.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to send copy of the Notices to the Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management submitted the required documents. Only P3,349,706.16 representing two (2) Notice of Suspensions, was settled on March 29, 2021. The Management failed to submit completely all documentary requirements as stated in the Notices of Suspensions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive thru the GAD Focal Person to exert efforts to fully implement the PPAs contained in its GAD Plan and Budget and require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed through local policy-making and integrated in the local development plans.
observations.managementAction: AAR The concerned adhered to the instruction of the LCE and they are beginning to prepare the task at hand.
observations.reason: The attribution of GAD-related PPAs of CY 2021 was not fully utilized. While, for the same period, the municipality has not yet formulate its GAD Code and ordinances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor admonish the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” on the payrolls, vouchers and its supporting documents upon release of payment so that double payment and other irregular use of paid vouchers will be avoided.
observations.managementAction: AAR Paid DVs were all stamped “Paid”.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a. Direct the members of the Local Finance Committee to carefully provide a realistic estimate of total estimated income in the preparation of Local Expenditure Program/Executive Budget through effective cash flow forecasting, being one of the Committee’s functions stated in Section 316 of Republic Act No. 7160; and b. Direct the Budget Officer to monitor and evaluate budget performance of all PPAs (financial and physical evaluation of actual results vs. targets) including from continuing appropriations, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Local Finance Committee has taken due notice of the observation of the audit team. Close monitoring and evaluation of budget performance was in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016; and (b) the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials and employees to: 1. Liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances; 2. Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; 3. Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advance to those who have unliquidated balances; and 4. Intensify collection to recover government funds invested in the program.
observations.managementAction: AAR The concerned officials prepared the liquidation of Accounts Receivable. Close monitoring of the liquidation of cash advances was in place. Collection was intensified. The municipality discontinued granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to update the CIPLC and draw reclassifying entry for completed projects based on information supplied by the Municipal Engineering Office and to adjust depreciation that have accumulated from the date of completion.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant was directed to update the CIPLC and to draw reclassifying entry for completed projects. The necessary coordination was done and the reclassification was being undertaken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the accountable officer to deposit all her collections daily and intact not later than the next banking day in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations to preclude intentional holding of cash that may result to possible loss or misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR The concerned accountable officer was directed to deposit collections daily and regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive admonish the Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate all his outstanding cash advances and if warranted institute appropriate action by withholding of his salary to ensure liquidation as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated Feb. 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Treasurer has been regularly liquidating his cash advances. The necessary actions were taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit quarterly the Report on SEF Utilization in order to monitor the implementation and operations of SEF.
observations.managementAction: AAR Utilization report for Special Education Fund was prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render his accounts and submit the required fiscal transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month. Likewise, remind accountable officers of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1445 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two (2) months.
observations.managementAction: AAR Concerned officials undertake to render accounts as supported by documents. The accountable officers were reminded of the sanctions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek appropriate funding for the titling the said parcel of land and include it on their annual or supplemental budget.
observations.managementAction: AAR There was sufficient funding for titling the lots and the necessary processing of titling was included in the budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents and rectify the deficiencies noted. Henceforth, submit annually the Subsidiary Ledgers, necessary schedules, supporting documents for the entries in the General Journal and strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR General Journals were supported with necessary documents. The Municipal Accountant submitted the supporting documents and rectified the deficiencies. Henceforth comply with PPSAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary adjusting entry to properly record the procurement of land and make a disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements on the unpaid balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant prepared the necessary adjusting entries and supporting documents. The necessary disclosure was made in the notes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should refrain from charging expenditures to fund transfers unless duly supported with appropriate documentation and to rectify the deficiencies noted and submit the supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management rectified the deficiencies and submitted the supporting documents. The outstanding balance of issued Notices of Suspension covering CY 2016 transactions was reduced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should refrain from procurement thru reimbursements and strictly implement the provisions of RA 9184 and related issuances and ensure procurement are supported with complete supporting documents and properly filled supporting documents including Drivers trip tickets and Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management implemented the recommendations and discontinued reimbursements. Drivers trip tickets were attached to fuel expenditures. The required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were prepared and submitted. Procurement and payments were supported with necessary documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders within five (5) days after their execution/issuance and copies of delivery documents within twenty-four (24) hours after acceptance.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Treasurer was required to submit copies of approved contracts/POs within five (5) days after execution and copies of delivery documents within 24 hours after acceptance as provided for in COA Circulars.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Accountant copies of the certified list of taxpayers and the corresponding assessed values of the taxable real property units to set up receivables at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS.
observations.managementAction: AAR Collection of real property has been based on the assessed value of the real property. The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Accountant the list and assessed values to facilitate the setting up of receivables at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required reconciliations and disclose either on the face of the Statement of Cash Flows/ SCBAA or in the Notes to Financial Statements in compliance with the provisions of IPSAS 2 and IPSAS 24 to achieve transparency in financial reporting.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to prepare the two reconciliation statements for CY 2020 financial statements.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the two reconciliation statements to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise due care in the of payments for every transaction, and to stamp “PAID” on the payrolls, vouchers and its supporting documents upon release of payment so that double payment and other irregular use of paid vouchers will be avoided.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commits to put stamp “paid” on the face of all paid vouchers, payrolls, and supporting documents.
observations.reason: No “paid” stamps were found on the face of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor the following courses of actions:

Direct the members of the Local Finance Committee to carefully provide a realistic estimate of total estimated income in the preparation of Local Expenditure Program/Executive Budget through effective cash flow forecasting, being one of the Committee’s functions stated in Section 316 of Republic Act No. 7160; and

Direct the Budget Officer to monitor and evaluate budget performance of all PPAs (financial and physical evaluation of actual results vs. targets) including from continuing appropriations, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee has taken due notice of the observation of the audit team.
observations.reason: The management has unutilized current appropriation in CY 2020 of ₱30,771, 942.00, which shows evidence that careful budget planning was not made. Further, a ₱20,000,000.00 beginning cash balance was estimated in CY 2020, without supporting computation, casting doubt if such amount is a realistic amount or not.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LCE require the Municipal Accountant to update the CIPLC and draw reclassifying entry for completed projects based on information supplied by the Municipal Engineering Office and adjusting entry for depreciation that have accumulated counting from the date of completion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already updated the CIPLC.
observations.reason: Construction in Progress Ledger Cards (CIPLCs) were not yet maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek appropriate funding for the titling the said parcel of land and include it on their annual or supplemental budget.
observations.reason: Certificate of Titles of the land amounting to ₱331,915.00 were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books to correct all erroneous recording or classification of items in PPE accounts.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant:
To provide an allowance for depreciation on all depreciable assets;
To prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the financial statements, and copies of the JEVs be furnished to the Audit Team for purposes of verification; and
To periodically reconcile the amounts recorded in the financial statement balance and in the PPE Ledger Cards of all PPE accounts.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to correct the accounting errors in the financial statements. Also, to submit the JEVs to record the correcting entries to the Audit Team for verification purposes.
We further recommended that a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) be executed between the LGU and the schools that will require the latter to submit Fund Utilization Reports to ensure that the funds transferred were utilized for the specified purpose.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant promised that a demand for the liquidation of the P100,000.00 grant to secondary schools, will be made. She further promised that correcting entries will be made on the erroneous recording of the transactions.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Municipal Accountant In-Charge and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the current year unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end; and
The Municipal Accountant In-Charge to follow the prescribed format in the disclosure of amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant disclosed that the transfer to Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management, was effected in CY 2021. Also, she promised that the prescribed format of the disclosure for LDRRMF will be followed.
The Audit Team will validate the alleged transfer upon our audit of the CY 2021 transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
appoint/designate a Supply and Property Officer or General Service Officer who shall maintain Property Cards;
create an Inventory Committee in accordance with Section 156 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments prescribed by COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, and direct said committee to conduct properly the required physical count of all PPEs of the LGU; and
require the Municipal Accountant to maintain Local Road Networks Ledger Card.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Administrator disclosed that the conduct of the physical count of PPEs is the priority of the LGU. Hence, he promises that the audit recommendation relative thereto will be complied with.
The Municipal Accountant, on the other hand, promised that the audit recommendations to be effected by her office, will also be complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to the following courses of actions:
identify the breakdown of P748,760.30 balance from CY 2019 and prior years’ transactions;
remit to the BIR the withholding taxes from all sources, regularly and intact, to avoid the accumulation of the same at year-end; and
match the BIR remittances against withholdings recorded in the General Ledger and Journals on a monthly basis to prevent under or over-remittances of taxes.
The Municipal Accountant promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee Chairperson, in coordination with BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant, to submit the lacking documentary requirements as provided in Appendix E.2 of this AOM, in compliance to GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020 and GPPB Resolution No. 18-2020 dated September 16, 2020, respectively.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer promised to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Sangguniang Bayan Presiding Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124 dated March 26, 2020 and to Budget Operations Manual for LGUs in effecting changes in the annual budget to fund COVID-19-related PPAs.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Budget Officer reasoned that the DBM only provided for a list of recipient-municipalities, of the Bayanihan Grant, thus, this list was no longer attached to the Appropriation Ordinance.
Also, she alleged that there was a confusion in the usage of terminologies related to changes in the Annual Budget, thus, resulted to erroneous means that the changes to the Annual Budget were made.
In fine however, she promised to comply with the audit recommendation.
The audit team maintained that the list provided by DBM was in lieu of the Statement of Funding Source and is considered an official communication that the LGU was a recipient of grant from the National Government, thus, it should be attached to the Appropriation Ordinance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO to submit the following:
Pictures taken during the receipt of the SAP by the beneficiaries;
Original or certified true copies of certifications issued by the respective Punong Barangays;
Relevant document evidencing the inclusion to a vulnerable sector of the non-resident beneficiaries; and
Duly dated, signed, and notarized Memorandum of Agreement by and between the municipality and DSWD Regional Director.
During the exit conference, the MSWDO reasoned that her office was not able to take pictures during the receipt by the beneficiaries of the SAP. Instead, only a group photo was taken. However, she revealed that the required taking of photo during the actual receipt was made in the second release of SAP.
She promised that the other audit recommendations will be complied with.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MENRO Designate to enforce proper handling of collected wastes at the central Materials Recovery Facility, and maintain an efficient and safe storage of the same pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003 for the protection of the environment, the general public and especially those directly handling the solid waste in the facility.
During the exit conference, the MENRO-Designate disclosed that the clearing of the Central MRF premises already started.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take the following courses of actions:
Require the designated Supply Officer to maintain stock cards for all types of inventories, including the receipt and issuance of relief goods/items for distributions, and to weekly prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to consolidate Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS);
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare supplies ledger cards for all types of inventories; and
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct semestral physical count of inventories.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Budget Officer admitted that it has been the practice of the municipality to use the forms presented above as this facilitates the monitoring of the movements of inventories through the First-In, First-Out method.
While, the Municipal Accountant asserted that her office cannot monitor the movements of the welfare goods, thus, ledger cards for this type of inventory is of no help.
The Supply Officer-Designate, however, committed that she will maintain the needed stock cards for all types of inventories.
The audit team maintained that all types of inventories must be provided with ledger cards.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The Municipal Accountant In-Charge to ensure complete documentation on all disbursements; and
The Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of title of these lots in favor of the municipality.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Assessor disclosed that processing of the requirements for the titling of subject parcels of land have started. Some of which includes the survey and payment of capital gains tax which were already finished. He promised that the documents for titling, once complete, will be immediately submitted to the Register of Deeds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to the following courses of action:
Ensure that the cost of signboards was included in the project cost, provided such conform to the specifications set forth under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
Require the contractor to post signboard, tarpaulins or similar materials as soon as the award has been made and must be suitably framed for outdoor display at the project location.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer revealed that his office already communicated with the contractors for the latter to post the necessary project signboards. Moreover, pictures of the postings thereof will be submitted to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Administrator to the following courses of actions:
Require all drivers of motor vehicles to properly fill-in the entries required in the Driver’s Trip Tickets and to prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels for each motor vehicle;
Direct the personnel in-charge to serially number the Driver’s Trip Tickets issued and prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle; and
Require the Bobon PNP to prepare and submit Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Report of Official Travels to the Municipality to monitor the fuel consumptions of their patrol cars.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Administrator promised to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to facilitate the implementation of PPAs funded by the LDRRMF.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the District Supervisor to optimize the utilization of Special Education Fund Budget pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 dated January 19, 2017, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2020 (Addendum No. 1) and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2, series of 2020 (Addendum No. 2).
During the exit conference, the Municipal Administrator explained that the SEF budget was not fully utilized due to over-estimation of PPAs under this fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to install measures necessary to safeguard the commercial units from theft, vandals and other bad elements.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer promised that the window provisions will be installed with temporary grills to protect the commercial units from intruders. Also, other measures will be installed to safeguard the terminal.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following courses of action:

To direct the Municipal Treasurer to confirm with Land Bank of the Philippines as to the status of LBP Account No. 1192-1230-93;

To direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully prepare bank reconciliations by properly applying the correct accounting treatment of bank/book reconciling items in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles; and

observations.recommendation:

The LDRRMO to prepare and submit monthly the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the same within the reglementary period; and

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended CY 2019 LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund; pursuant to NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.reason: The MDRRMO failed to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in a monthly basis to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to follow-up with the NSWMC the resolution on the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan, and submit a copy of the approved plan to COA. observations.recommendation:

 Reconcile the amounts of BIR taxes withheld and remitted for CY 2019 and prior years;

Use the last day of the taxable month as the cut-off date for remittance in order to ensure remittance of all withheld taxes within the same taxable month; and

Maintain subsidiary ledgers per tax type for Due to BIR account to monitor and prevent the accumulation of unpaid withheld taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to exert effort to reconcile the taxes withheld and remitted, and to observe the month-end cut-off date for BIR remittance purposes. Further, she committed to provide the subsidiary ledgers for Due to BIR account.

Further, the Audit Team found an accumulation of ₱27,167.63 Due to BIR balance from CY 2020 transactions that requires reconciliation. Moreover, subsidiary ledgers were not yet maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Local Chief Executive thru the GAD Focal Person to exert efforts to fully implement the PPAs contained in its GAD Plan and Budget to realize its intended purpose and require the Sanggunian to formulate and pass a local GAD code or ordinance with the assistance of the LGU GFPS, to support the localization of Magna Carta of Women and to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in local policy-making and integrated in local development plans.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted a revised 2019 GAD accomplishment report, as there were items that were not reflected in the initially submitted report. Further, the management will also recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan to draft their own GAD code.
observations.reason: Copy of GAD Code/ Ordinance was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:

the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books and if warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016; and

the Local Chief Executive direct all concerned officials and employees to:

Liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances by compelling them to submit liquidation papers and refund the unused cash advances, if any;

Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period;

Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advance to those who have unliquidated balances; and

Intensify collection to recover government funds invested in the program.

The concerned Officials prepared the liquidation of the Accounts Receivable.

observations.recommendation:
Since fiscal responsibility rests upon the Head of the Agency, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to render her accounts and submit the required financial transactions together with supporting documents to the Accounting Division, and the Municipal Accountant to submit the accounts and reports to COA on or before the 10th day of the succeeding month.
Likewise, the accountable officer and/or other officials concerned are reminded of the sanctions imposed under Sec. 2 of PD 1455 in relation to Art. 218 of the Revised Penal Code on the automatic suspension of payment of salaries for failure to render accounts in a period of two months.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should admonish the Local Accountant and Budget Officer to be responsive to their duties and responsibilities. The maintenance and recording of appropriation and allotment received, obligation incurred and disbursed is a must to prevent incurrence of obligations and disbursements in excess of allotment received.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustment for the stale checks to reflect the correct balances of the cash accounts;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit timely the bank reconciliation statements with the bank statement and original copies of paid checks as prescribed under Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Submit the list of deposits in transit and other items for adjustment by the bank, indicated in the BRS, and the negotiated and cancelled checks pursuant to the pertinent paragraph 3.0 and 3.5 of COA Circular 96-011 to facilitate the review on the accuracy of the submitted BRS.

We recommend that the Municipal School Board be directed to prepare the Annual School Board Budget for the ensuing fiscal year to be approved by all the members of the board and duly supported with programs, projects and activities. The Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit the Quarterly and Annual Report of SEF utilization aligned with Section 99 and 100 of RA 7160 and DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 2 of 2017.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to institute action for the collection of the accounts or other appropriate remedies to clear the amount in the books;
Analyze each fund transfer account balance and determine the reason why these were not liquidated and undertake immediate appropriate action thereon; and
If warranted, request/apply for the write-off of the accounts following the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2016-005, dated December 19, 2016.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials and employees to:
a) Liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances by compelling them to submit liquidation papers and refund the unused cash advances, if any;
b) Monitor closely the liquidation of outstanding cash advances by sending written reminder/demand on the submission of liquidation report within the reglementary period; and
c) Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to those who have unliquidated balances.

We recommend strict observance to the provision of Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 requiring submission of the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA auditor of the LGU.
We recommend that the positions required under the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 be filled up immediately by the Municipality to properly institutionalize the organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office.
We recommend that management observe the accounting and reporting guidelines.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that only expenditures/ projects numerated under the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 are considered in the charging against the 20% Development Fund and
Strictly utilize the fund in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the JMC to fully achieve the intention and purpose for which it was established.
Restore to the 20% Development Fund the ineligible expenditures that were improperly charged out of the same fund amounting to P2,173,097.04.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:
Give priority for the creation of an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE.
Direct Head of the General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office to hasten the updating of their records to facilitate the reconciliation of the accounting and property records.
Oblige the Municipal Accountant to review the details of properties which are non-functional or unserviceable and prepare the necessary adjustments, if warranted.
Require the committee to prepare a methodical and well planned physical inventory taking, which could be done in a year round basis, until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the municipality have been accounted for and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).  Also, all AREs must be renewed regularly pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
Thereafter, the RPCPPE must be reconciled with the property record of each class of property at the General Services Office and subsidiary ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office.  Any discrepancy should be investigated and appropriate action should be taken thereof.
Make representation with the previous accounting personnel to substantiate and reconcile the forwarded balances prior to CY 2012.
Work out and to see to it that Titles of the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the Municipal Government of San Isidro to establish absolute ownership thereof.
Direct strict compliance with the accounting and reporting guidelines for local road network construction and maintenance system prescribed by .COA Circular No. 2015-008;
Instruct the Accountants to draw a JEV to transfer the costs of completed projects from CIP-Infrastructure Assets and Building and Other Structures account to Infrastructure Assets and Building/School building or other structures account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the supporting documents for some entries in the General Journal and rectify the deficiencies noted. Henceforth, submit annually the Subsidiary Ledgers, necessary schedules, supporting documents for the entries in the General Journal and strictly comply the provisions of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the preparation of the financial statements.
